nodes.label: trade-journal
nodes.summary: A command-line trade journal that quantifies the execution leak between plan-traded and realized results.

nodes.label: Execution Leak
nodes.summary: The gap between what setups should have produced (plan-traded) and what was actually banked (realized), expressed as a number to watch shrink.

nodes.label: journal command
nodes.summary: The CLI command provided by installing the package, used to add, view stats, and list trades.

nodes.label: journal add
nodes.summary: Logs a trade, either interactively or non-interactively with flags like --setup, --direction, --risk, --rr, --result.

nodes.label: journal stats
nodes.summary: Produces the performance and leak report; supports a --since date filter.

nodes.label: journal list
nodes.summary: Lists recent trades, with -n to limit the count shown.

nodes.label: Trade
nodes.summary: A recorded trade capturing setup, timeframe, direction, risk, planned R:R, result, target_hit, and behaviour flags.

nodes.label: target_hit
nodes.summary: A field indicating whether price reached the planned target; this is what unlocks the leak calculation.

nodes.label: dragged_stop
nodes.summary: A behaviour flag marking trades where the stop was dragged, a primary attributed cause of leak.

nodes.label: out_of_plan
nodes.summary: A behaviour flag for trades taken outside the plan, an attributed cause of leak.

nodes.label: reversal_zone
nodes.summary: A behaviour flag indicating entry into a SIBI / reversal zone before target.

nodes.label: Missed Setup
nodes.summary: A setup not taken, recorded with the R you'd have made, used to compute missed-setup opportunity cost.
nodes.source_location: README.md (What it records per trade / report)

nodes.label: Performance & Leak Report
nodes.summary: Report comparing realized win rate vs setup hit rate, realized R vs plan-traded R, leak attribution, and missed-setup opportunity cost.

nodes.label: data/trades.csv
nodes.summary: Human-readable, git-ignored CSV file where trades are stored; relocatable via TRADE_JOURNAL_DB.

nodes.label: TRADE_JOURNAL_DB
nodes.summary: Environment variable to point the journal's data storage to an alternate CSV file path.

nodes.label: Python 3.10+ (standard library)
nodes.summary: The tool requires Python 3.10+ and uses only the standard library, with no third-party dependencies.

nodes.label: pytest / unittest
nodes.summary: Tests are run with python3 -m pytest or python3 -m unittest.

nodes.label: R (risk multiple)
nodes.summary: Unit of risk-normalized return used throughout for measuring planned R:R, realized R, and leak.
